inputs.userGoal: Build a secure ML training pipeline with data stored in Azure Data Lake, audit logging for all training jobs, and encryption for all outputs.

architecturePrinciples: Secure by default — CMK encryption on all storage, RBAC authorization on Key Vault
Full auditability — Log Analytics captures all ML job telemetry and resource diagnostics
Data Lake separation — dedicated ADLS Gen2 for training data, separate default storage for ML workspace artifacts
Least privilege — User Assigned Managed Identity scoped to required resources only

resources.name: log-mltraining-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Central audit log sink for all ML training job telemetry, resource diagnostics, and security events. Supports KQL queries for compliance auditing. 90-day retention balances cost and audit requirements.
alternativesConsidered: CapacityReservation SKU — not needed at initial scale
Azure Sentinel — overkill for training job audit logging
reasoning.tradeoffs: PerGB2018 is cost-efficient at low-to-moderate volume; consider CapacityReservation at 100+ GB/day.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No upstream dependencies — foundational observability resource.

resources.name: appi-mltraining-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Workspace-based Application Insights feeds into Log Analytics for unified audit logging of ML workspace activity. Required dependency of the ML workspace for job telemetry and monitoring.
alternativesConsidered: Classic Application Insights — deprecated, no Log Analytics integration
Skip App Insights entirely — loses ML workspace built-in monitoring
reasoning.tradeoffs: Pricing determined by Log Analytics workspace; no separate App Insights cost with workspace-based mode.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Workspace-based App Insights requires a Log Analytics workspace for data storage.

resources.name: id-mltraining-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Required for CMK encryption at storage account creation time. System-assigned identity cannot be used for CMK on new storage accounts. Also used by the ML workspace for secure data access.
alternativesConsidered: System-assigned identity — cannot be used for CMK at storage creation
Service principal — managed identities eliminate credential management
reasoning.tradeoffs: Adds one extra resource but enables zero-credential encryption key management.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No upstream dependencies — identity resource created first for downstream CMK references.

resources.name: kv-mltraining-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Central encryption key management for CMK on both storage accounts and the ML workspace. Soft-delete and purge protection required for CMK. RBAC authorization preferred over legacy access policies for security.
alternativesConsidered: Premium SKU with HSM-backed keys — not required unless regulatory mandates HSM
Azure Managed HSM — overkill for standard ML training encryption needs
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard SKU uses software-protected keys; upgrade to Premium if HSM-backed keys are mandated by compliance.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No upstream dependencies — foundational security resource.

resources.name: stmltrainingprod001

reasoning.whyChosen: Default storage for ML workspace — stores job outputs, models, snapshots, and logs. CMK encryption via Key Vault ensures all outputs are encrypted with customer-managed keys. StorageV2 required by ML workspace pairing constraint. Network ACLs default deny for security.
alternativesConsidered: Standard_GRS for geo-redundancy — adds cost, not required for training artifacts
Standard_ZRS for zone redundancy — consider for production-critical workloads
reasoning.tradeoffs: LRS provides single-region redundancy. Upgrade to ZRS/GRS if higher durability is required.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Key Vault provides CMK encryption keys. User-assigned identity required for CMK at storage creation.

resources.name: stdatalakeprod001

reasoning.whyChosen: ADLS Gen2 (HNS-enabled) for hierarchical file system access to training datasets. CMK encryption ensures data-at-rest security. Separate from ML workspace default storage to isolate training data lifecycle and access control. Network ACLs default deny for security.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Blob Storage without HNS — loses hierarchical namespace and ABFS driver performance
Azure Data Lake Storage Gen1 — deprecated, not recommended for new workloads
reasoning.tradeoffs: HNS cannot be disabled after creation. ADLS Gen2 does not support NFS 3.0 with GRS failover if geo-redundancy is needed later.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Key Vault provides CMK encryption keys. User-assigned identity required for CMK at storage creation.

references.title: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 overview
Enable HNS for Data Lake Storage

resources.name: mlw-mltraining-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Central ML workspace for training pipeline orchestration. HBI mode enables enhanced data protection. Links to Key Vault for CMK, App Insights for audit telemetry, and default storage for encrypted outputs. Managed network with AllowInternetOutbound balances security and usability for pulling training packages.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Databricks — stronger for Spark-based ETL but heavier for pure ML training
AllowOnlyApprovedOutbound isolation — more secure but requires explicit outbound rules for every dependency
reasoning.tradeoffs: Basic SKU is cost-effective for training workloads. AllowInternetOutbound permits package installs; tighten to AllowOnlyApprovedOutbound for stricter network control.

resources.dependencyReasoning: ML workspace requires linked Storage Account, Key Vault, and Application Insights at creation. User-assigned identity provides CMK and data access.

overallReasoning.summary: This architecture provisions a secure Azure Machine Learning training pipeline with seven resources organized around three pillars: (1) Security — Key Vault manages CMK encryption keys, both storage accounts use customer-managed encryption, RBAC is enforced throughout, and the ML workspace runs in HBI mode. (2) Auditability — Log Analytics Workspace collects all diagnostics and ML job telemetry via workspace-based Application Insights, providing KQL-queryable audit trails. (3) Data management — a dedicated ADLS Gen2 storage account (HNS-enabled) holds training datasets, while a separate StorageV2 account stores ML workspace artifacts and encrypted outputs.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: LRS storage is single-region only — upgrade to ZRS or GRS for higher durability. AllowInternetOutbound network isolation permits outbound internet access for package installs — tighten to AllowOnlyApprovedOutbound for zero-trust. Standard Key Vault uses software keys — upgrade to Premium for HSM-backed keys if compliance requires it.

plan.validation: All resources deploy to eastus. ML workspace links to its required dependencies (Storage, Key Vault, App Insights) which are immutable after creation. Both storage accounts use StorageV2 kind and CMK via Key Vault with purge protection enabled. App Insights is workspace-based, backed by Log Analytics. Dependency ordering ensures identity and Key Vault exist before storage accounts, and all linked resources exist before the ML workspace.
